subject: Ivanka Trump calls on American companies to invest in our workers
body:
For decades, American workers were neglected and forgotten as Washington stood by idly. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e349a11d44&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • March 6, 2019
---
| | |
# **Ivanka Trump calls on American companies to invest in our workers**

**For decades,** American workers were neglected and forgotten as Washington stood by idly. While corporate America pushed for cheap labor abroad, our leaders let communities in the heartland crumble instead of fighting for blue-collar employees.

**President Donald J. Trump** pledged a new kind of economic agenda to change all of that. In just over two years in office, his Buy American and Hire American policies have poured investment back into the United States. More manufacturing jobs were created in 2018 than any single year in the last 20, for example.

**America now has the hottest economy** on earth. The next step is making sure that soaring growth pays off for every working American family.

**Today,****Advisor to the President Ivanka Trump** hosted some of the country’s top CEOs—including Apple’s Tim Cook and Walmart’s Doug McMillon—for the first meeting of the American Workforce Policy Advisory Board at the White House. The group, which will advise President Trump’s National Council for the American Worker, is made up of 25 leaders from across industries and communities.

**Its mission** : Help more American workers get the skills training they need to fill one of the 7.3 million available jobs in our country today.

**“Employers were coming to us** and they were saying, ‘We’re optimistic about America, we want to invest here and a constraint for growth is the lack of a skilled workforce,’” Ms. Trump told _[The Wall Street Journal](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=48a8b49c68&e=3ee4985eea)_ this week. “We don’t have people to fill the jobs.”

**The Pledge to America’s Workers** , which President Trump announced last summer and Ms. Trump leads, is part of a major push by the Trump Administration to fix that. Since the launch last July, 205 companies and organizations have pledged more than 6.5 _million_ new career opportunities and apprenticeships for American workers and students.

**“The right skills matter** more than degrees,” Ms. Trump said.

[6.5 million and counting: The Pledge to America’s Workers gains steam](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=0fb9a4d774&e=3ee4985eea)

**Watch** : [Ivanka Trump kicks off the first meeting of the advisory board](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=82451b7b76&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## **Quote of the day: Record numbers of illegal migrants**

**“Bottom line** is that if this continues, it’s like adding a congressional district of illegal immigrants every year,” Sen. Lindsey Graham (R-SC) said of America’s border crisis.

**He’s right.** The average congressional district is home to about 600,000 people. According to numbers released yesterday by the Department of Homeland Security, officials have apprehended nearly 268,000 people at the southwest border since October 1—on target to reach more than 640,000 for the fiscal year.

**“This has to stop,”** Sen. Graham says.

**Homeland Security Secretary Kirstjen Nielsen** delivered that same message to Congress today. “We face a crisis—a real, serious, and sustained crisis at our borders,” she said. “I hope there can be a consensus that the current system isn’t working and that this is an emergency.”

**Among the horrible stories** of victims suffering under the current, lawless system, [one told by Secretary Nielsen](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=fef49eb3bf&e=3ee4985eea) today should move every member of Congress to act. Young women traveling north to the U.S. border are sexually abused with such regularity, she said, that U.S. immigration officials must give every girl over age 10 a pregnancy test after arriving.

**“This is not a safe journey,”** she added. No one should defend this awful status quo.

[Press Secretary Sarah Sanders: Even former President Obama called this a crisis](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=075e41ec14&e=3ee4985eea)

**By the numbers:** [Our border crisis grows worse by the day](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6f8bc50bf9&e=3ee4985eea)

name: Jess Fassler
subject: Our March to-do list
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Gillibrand 2020 Exploratory Committee
body:
We just set new action items for March, and I want to loop you in.
Peter, I promised from the beginning I'd share every step of this campaign with you. Every plan, every goal, every loss and every victory. Because we're in this together.
**So here 's where we stand as of today:** We've got big goals in March to recruit volunteers and get our message to Democratic voters. But in order to make this happen, we need 10,000 more donors to give to fund our campaign plan for the month--and I can't stomach pulling back anywhere:
1. **We have to keep Kirsten on the road.** When she's in a room with people, something magical happens. The connections run deep. We walk away with a whole bunch of new voters and volunteers. Next up: We're heading back to Iowa and New Hampshire and taking our first trips to Michigan and Nevada. But as important as these trips are, they're also expensive--and staying on budget is how we fund them.
2. **We have to build up our voter outreach efforts in early primary states.** We've signed up so many supporters over the last month, but it's a wasted opportunity without the resources to organize them. We need to keep adding staff and volunteers so that come Caucus Day in Iowa and the primary in New Hampshire, we know our people--and have the team in place to turn them out.
3. **We need to keep up our digital ads.** It's too early to spend on TV, but one of the best ways to grow our team is with online ads. We need to get Kirsten's message out all over the internet--because that's where a modern campaign maximizes its support. We're telling people what we stand for: a Green New Deal, Medicare for All, the most anti-Trump record in the Senate. If we get this record in front of people, our support will grow and we'll go the distance.

Campaigns are complex, but they aren't rocket science. If we do what I've outlined above, we'll be in strong shape going into the next quarter, where this race only gets tougher.
Now's the moment when your gift will make its greatest impact. We need 10,000 more donors by the reporting deadline at the end of the month, when our numbers go public and the press will report on who's hitting their marks and who's falling short.
Peter, all that said: Can you help us with a gift now? We're doing this without money from corporate PACs, federal lobbyists, super PACs or establishment megadonors--so we need YOUR support.

### [The Democrats Go Fishing](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtkhkdkk-kujkulhluu-i/)
The Democrats have set off on one of the biggest fishing expeditions in American history. The House Judiciary Committee, which is now under Democrat control, is demanding documents from 81 people who somehow have connections with Donald Trump.

Click the link below to read more and hear my thoughts. [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtkhkdkk-kujkulhluu-d/) _Previous Weekly Posts_ [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtkhkdkk-kujkulhluu-h/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtkhkdkk-kujkulhluu-k/)
### [Should the Mueller Report be Released to the Public?](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtkhkdkk-kujkulhluu-u/)
Whenever Robert Mueller finishes up his Russian collusion investigation (likely to be this week or next), he will submit his confidential report to our new Attorney General, William Barr. Then Barr will have a decision to make. How much of the report does he release to Congress and how much does he release to the public?

Click the link below to read more and hear my thoughts. [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtkhkdkk-kujkulhluu-o/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtkhkdkk-kujkulhluu-b/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtkhkdkk-kujkulhluu-n/)
### [An Evening With Ulysses S. Grant](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtkhkdkk-kujkulhluu-p/)
I considered writing this week's blog about President Trump's declaration of a national emergency, or which Democrat jumped into the presidential race most recently, or AOC's latest crackpot proposal, but decided instead to write about Ulysses S. Grant. Yes, Ulysses S. Grant.

Click the link below to read more. [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtkhkdkk-kujkulhluu-x/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtkhkdkk-kujkulhluu-m/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtkhkdkk-kujkulhluu-c/)
### [So Just How Radical Is This New Democratic Party?](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtkhkdkk-kujkulhluu-q/)
Pretty darn radical. John F. Kennedy sure wouldn't recognize it. He believed instinctively in a strong America. In lower taxes to spur economic growth. In a robust foreign policy, and an adequate military to support it. In less government, not more. Remember "ask not what your country can do for you, ask what you can do for your country?" subject: SURVEY: What does socialism mean to you?
body:
| | It’s not always clear what socialism means.
---
[ ](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66iRh8hiUuPoSN_I-P-OsDnF3nuTESlC5CErQkt_5nVUEbFuE-5OAT5c5B9gD0A1H8aLoy848p-fr0kW0-p_mER5E&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190307srv01&n=1)
| Peter, With Democratic control of the House of Representatives, there is growing support for new legislation that would introduce additional social programs expanding the scope of the federal government. President Trump, in his State of the Union address last month, warned of socialism creeping into our nation. Democratic leaders, on the other hand, describe their policies as “democratic socialism,” suggesting their ideal is not Venezuela but the policies of countries like Sweden. While many Americans now support socially progressive policies, it’s not always clear if that directly translates to supporting socialism. **Do you think socialism is more of a threat or help to most Americans?** [](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66iRh8hiUuPoSN_I-P-OsDnF3nuTESlC5CErQkt_5nVUEbFuE-5OAT5c5B9gD0A1H8aHuhXGfiWePPYS3qWrzYKUg0tN70lty5QA6mwsfnqXkf2MLwcPt70I0s7uX2t2mfTHOwILqwz-A477WV8OoPAdA&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190307srv01&n=2) [](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66iRh8hiUuPoSN_I-P-OsDnF3nuTESlC5CErQkt_5nVUEbFuE-5OAT5c5B9gD0A1H8aHuhXGfiWePPYS3qWrzYKUg0tN70lty5QA6mwsfnqXkf2MLwcPt70I0s7uX2t2mfTLNUym98ERxv3ZJeMfYFYy4&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190307srv01&n=3) **United. Friend,

This week, the House is moving forward with our first piece of legislation since Democrats took over: H.R. 1, the For The People Act.

This legislation will help ensure free and fair elections, curb big money in politics, and clean up our ethics policies. In short: Democrats are getting to work for the American people.

[**_Will you join me in urging my colleagues on both sides of the aisle to pass this bill and help our democracy work for everyone?_**](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113c25/622b7b5b/1ec39ded/307b7153/142931979/VEsE/ "https://debbiewassermanschultz.com/landing/for-the-people/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=schultz&utm_content=2+-+Will+you+join+me+in+urging+my+colleagues&utm_campaign=DWS_EM_PT_90DO_190307_1_1&source=DWS_EM_PT_90DO_190307_1_1")

[](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113c25/622b7b5b/1ec39ded/307b7152/142931979/VEsF/ "https://debbiewassermanschultz.com/landing/for-the-people/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=schultz&utm_content=3&utm_campaign=DWS_EM_PT_90DO_190307_1_1&source=DWS_EM_PT_90DO_190307_1_1")
When Democrats took back the House, we made a commitment to make Washington work for everyone, not just for the special interests. This bill is a major step in that process, and there's no reason it shouldn't pass. [**_Please sign on now to show your support for H.R. 1._**](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113c25/622b7b5b/1ec39ded/307b7155/142931979/VEsC/ "https://debbiewassermanschultz.com/landing/for-the-people/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=schultz&utm_content=4+-+Please+sign+on+now+to+show+your+support+&utm_campaign=DWS_EM_PT_90DO_190307_1_1&source=DWS_EM_PT_90DO_190307_1_1")

Thank you,

Debbie

Trump's presidency has been filled with a dizzying amount of corruption. Public leaders are supposed to put others first and work to make Americans' lives better, but he and his administration have tossed that role aside to do whatever serves their own best interests.
I'm running for president because I will always put the American people first. I'm running to restore what's been lost. I'm running to sweep out the corruption that touches every level of our government today.
**You deserve to know if your elected leaders are being bankrolled by federal lobbyists and putting their interests before yours. You deserve a government that 's listening, working and fighting to make your lives better. **
That's why I refuse to accept any support from corporate PACs, federal lobbyists or super PACs--and today, we can take a step toward making sure other candidates do the same. I just introduced legislation called the CLEAR Act to require campaigns to disclose gifts from registered lobbyists, and I'm asking you to add your name and show you stand against corrupt money in our politics:
**[We need you to be one of 800 supporters to join our fight before 11:59 p.m. tonight to demand transparency and accountability in our campaigns. Will you add your name right now?](https://secure.kirstengillibrand. "https://act.kirstengillibrand.com/CLEARact?firstName=Peter&lastName=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&code=jtk2863-su-gfp-nd")**
Since I was first elected to office, I've worked to make our politics more transparent. I was the first-ever member of Congress to post an online list of my official meetings, earmark requests and personal financial disclosures.
I'm running for president to keep fighting these fights. To make our economy fairer. To make sure women are heard and valued. To fight against those who've rigged our politics to favor the NRA and let special interests hand our economy to their wealthy clients.
And in this campaign, we've taken a stand from the outset: No corporate PACs, no gifts from federal lobbyists and no individual super PAC. And that's just our first step.
I am all-in on this fight. Returning power to the heart of our communities is what this election's about, and we just can't wait. The CLEAR Act would be a huge step toward weakening the power lobbyists have over politicians and policy.
**[Be one of 800 supporters to join this fight before 11:59 p.m. tonight by adding your name to demand transparency and accountability in our campaigns.](https://secure.kirstengillibrand. "https://act.kirstengillibrand.com/CLEARact?firstName=Peter&lastName=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&code=jtk2863-su-gfp-nd")**
We can make a difference, but only together. Thank you for being part of our team and standing alongside me to call for the transparency and accountability you deserve.
Kirsten

subject: ‘Being Anti-Trump Means Never Having to Say You Are Sorry’
body:
"All during the fight over a border wall, we were told by the media and progressives commentators that the border is practically locked down." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=92c2ee4fe2&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **‘Being Anti-Trump Means Never Having to Say You Are Sorry’**

“All during the fight over a border wall, we were told by the media and progressives commentators that the border is practically locked down,” Rich Lowry writes in _Politico Magazine_. But alarming new numbers from the Department of Homeland Security say otherwise: “More than 76,000 migrants were apprehended crossing the southern border last month, the highest February in more than 10 years,” Lowry explains.

“Being anti-Trump means never having to say you are sorry or you were wrong, so the latest evidence won’t make an impression on anyone who blithely dismissed the idea of border crisis. But it should . . . Every indication is that the situation is going to get worse.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=a143685da1&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “Homeland Security Secretary Kirstjen Nielsen said Wednesday that young female migrants traveling north to the United States are raped with such regularity that Immigration and Customs Enforcement gives every girl age 10 and over a pregnancy test after arriving,” David Martosko reports in the _[Daily Mail](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=5b2e164441&e=3ee4985eea)_.
---
|
---
| “Chief executives of major companies said at a White House forum on Wednesday that they are hiring more Americans without college degrees,” David Shepardson reports for _[Reuters](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=edff38b771&e=3ee4985eea)_. “The White House hosted CEOs of major corporations who joined a Trump administration advisory board on workforce issues, including from Apple Inc, IBM Corp, Lockheed Martin Corp, Siemens USA and Home Depot Inc, who are part of a 25-member board co-chaired by President Donald Trump’s daughter and adviser Ivanka Trump and Commerce Secretary Wilbur Ross.”
---
|
---
| “Democrats seem hell-bent on impeaching Mr. Trump, and most of the media will be cheering them on,” _[The Wall Street Journal](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=f03181c1eb&e=3ee4985eea)_ editorial board writes. House Judiciary Committee Chairman Jerrold Nadler’s (D-NY) subpoena swarm is meant “to turn the President’s exercise of his normal constitutional powers into impeachable offenses.”
---
|
---
| “Last Friday, President Trump unveiled his grand strategy for American prosperity: the 2019 Trade Policy Agenda. This forward-looking report from the US Trade Representative maps out America’s deliverance from the bad deals, weak guardrails and sluggish growth that have handicapped decades of international trade,” [White House Director for Trade and Manufacturing Policy Peter Navarro](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=be90194238&e=3ee4985eea) writes in the _New York Post_.
---
|
---
| “President Donald Trump made a wise choice in tapping Kentucky’s Kelly Craft as his new nominee for U.S. ambassador to the United Nations,” Scott Jennings writes in the _[Courier Journal](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=507bede965&e=3ee4985eea)_. “Craft is more than capable of representing America on the world stage,” which will be her third diplomatic post—and second stint at the United Nations.
---
|

name: Sherrod Brown
subject: Believe me, we will fight.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Sherrod Brown
body:
|
---

| | Peter, First, I want to say thank you. Thank you for your support as I ran for re-election in Ohio, and thank you for your support as we traveled the country on the Dignity of Work tour. **I want you to be the first to know that we 've decided the best place for me to continue fighting for the people of Ohio and for the dignity of all workers across the country is in the U.S. Senate. So, I will not run for President in 2020.** As you've heard me say many times, when you love this country, you fight for the people who make it work. I fight best when I bring joy to the battle. And I find that joy fighting for Ohio in the Senate. So, that's where I can be most effective. You know being President isn't something I've dreamed of my whole life. My goal for our tour was to make the dignity of work the centerpiece of the Democrats' 2020 campaign because I believe that's the way to beat Donald Trump, and it's the way we should govern. And we've done that. **Not only are other candidates starting to talk about the dignity of work, voters across the country are demanding it.** From the farmers we met in Iowa, to the childcare workers we met in New Hampshire, the hotel workers in Nevada and the people struggling to overcome homelessness in South Carolina -- it is clear the dignity of work is a value that unites all of us. And I will keep making sure dignity of work is front and center in the 2020 campaign as I fight for it every day in the U.S. Senate. So as I think about the work we have left to do -- from making sure truck drivers and coal miners get the retirement they earned, to combating addiction, to helping the workers who were laid off in Lordstown -- I am convinced the best place for me to make that fight is in the Senate. **And believe me, we will fight.** I will continue calling out President Trump's phony populism and holding him accountable for betraying workers in Ohio and across the country. And I will do everything I can to elect a Democratic President and a Democratic Senate in 2020. Thank you, Peter. Onward, Sherrod
---

name: Bernie Sanders
subject: I am writing to ask if you could make a $3 donation to our campaign. We have an important FEC reporting deadline coming up and our job is to generate as many individual donations between now and the time it comes to a close.
body:
Here's the truth:
# [](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/8915?t=1&akid=688%2E150750%2EZDTCAJ)

Sisters and Brothers -
In just a moment, I am going to ask you to make a donation to our campaign. Please give me a moment to explain why this ask is so important.
Here is the truth: we have gotten off to a better start than most in the corporate media and political establishment thought possible, but we have an enormous amount of work in front of us and the path forward will not be easy.
The wealthy and the powerful will do everything they can to defend their financial interests, and they have an unlimited amount of money at their disposal.
But what we did on our last campaign, and what we’ll have to do again, is raise what we need to win from a lot of people giving small donations. Because I am not going to be traveling the country begging millionaires and billionaires for donations.
That means from time to time we have to ask you to help. And today is one of those days:
[**Can you make a $3 donation to our campaign today? Our first official FEC fundraising deadline is right around the corner and we have some big goals to hit to keep our campaign on pace to win.**](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/9823?refcode=em190308-1-120-full&t=32&akid=688%2E150750%2EZDTCAJ)
When this FEC fundraising deadline comes to a close, we’re going to report not just how much money we’ve raised, but how many donations are powering that number. And in my view, it is important that we show as many individual donations as we possibly can. That is the number that matters most to me.
In solidarity,
Bernie Sanders
[DONATE](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/9823?refcode=em190308-1-120-full&t=33&akid=688%2E150750%2EZDTCAJ)

Paid for by Bernie 2020
PO BOX 391, Burlington, VT 05402
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you need to, [unsubscribe using this link](https://bernie.actionkit.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=34&akid=688%2E150750%2EZDTCAJ). But we hope you'll stay, because emails are such an important part of how we organize and how we raise money. In fact, there is no single greater source for our campaign's fundraising than emails like this one. And that's important, because no one person, not even Bernie Sanders, can take on Trump and the billionaire class alone.

Before you go about the rest of your day, help power our fight against the billionaire class by [making a contribution today](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bernie-email-template?refcode=em-footer&t=35&refcode2=688_150750_ZDTCAJ&akid=688%2E150750%2EZDTCAJ).
